what_it_does: This file is a context brief that describes an existing phone-driven annotation/feedback system and the substrate of already-running code/design primitives that surround it.
observations: It is labeled as a CLIENT BRIEF for a mobile review/annotation system.
It is doc #2 in the dragnet-development channel of 'the Company'.
It describes an existing mobile review surface served at https://workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net:8790/ via ops/doc_review_server.py.
Board documents are rendered as ordered, addressable blocks that can be tapped and commented on.
Comments are stored as typed edges (commented_on / reply_to) on the board.
It inventories existing building blocks: ~19 address schemes, board items with typed edges, append-only marks, scopes, channels/membership, blob store, and ui:// / code:// addresses.
It notes there are three parallel 'channel' systems and that membership gates message delivery, not data access.
It mentions the Company UI already tags rendered elements with ui:// addresses and code archaeology produces code:// cards, enabling a UI element → source trace.
standouts: A live mobile review server already exists and is reachable from Tim's iPhone over Tailscale.
UI elements are already addressable via ui:// and traceable to code:// source cards.
State is composed from append-only marks rather than stored mutations ('mark-is-truth').
There are three parallel channel systems, which is flagged as a notable caveat.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Markdown body with a single top-level heading and explanatory prose.
Uses board:// addresses and typed link kinds such as part_of.
References external files and URLs as concrete anchors.
Written as a self-contained context document for other AI sessions.
concerns: The existence of three parallel channel systems is flagged as a potential architectural ambiguity.
Membership gates delivery rather than access, leaving access-control semantics unclear.
The file references live URLs and external file paths that cannot be verified from the file alone.
It does not describe security, authentication, or authorization for the review endpoint.
notes: A context brief that maps out the existing primitives for a phone-based annotation system so later design work builds on real seams rather than starting from scratch.
questions: What exactly distinguishes the three parallel 'channel' systems?
How are comment/reply edges ordered, threaded, and rendered?
What does 'conducted' coordinator mode mean operationally?
How is ui:// to code:// resolution implemented in practice?
What does 'mark-is-truth' imply for conflict resolution or state rollback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide context for what exists and the world it lives in; the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Addressability of every artefact via a unified resolver.
Append-only observations as the source of truth.
Multi-agent coordination over a shared disk-backed store.
Traceability from rendered UI elements to editable source code.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared, concrete baseline for designing or researching a mobile annotation system by cataloguing existing capabilities and constraints so proposals extend real infrastructure.
novelty: The notion of a fabric of isolated Claude Code sessions coordinating through a shared disk-backed store, a single resolver for ~19 address schemes, append-only marks composing state, and a pre-existing UI-to-source traceability chain via ui:// and code:// addresses.
contribution: A context inventory of the existing annotation/feedback system and its surrounding primitives, including the address model, board graph, marks, scopes, channels, blob store, and UI/code traceability hooks, which a fused system could use as a baseline for further design.
summary_for_embedding: A context brief in the dragnet-development channel describing an existing iPhone-accessible mobile review/annotation server and the surrounding addressable board, mark, scope, channel, blob, and UI-to-code substrate.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a shared baseline of existing capabilities and constraints for designing a phone-driven annotation and feedback system.
